title: Week 09 - Search and Binary Search
description: Practice linear search, position search, search-count statistics, and binary search.

questions.title: Search a Number-Card Array for a Target Student

description.background: The teacher stores student number cards for an activity in the array numbers. Now the teacher needs to confirm whether student number target is in the list.
description.task: Search the array to decide whether target exists.
description.input: One array numbers, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return true if target is found; otherwise return false.
description.hint: You can start from the first position and check one item at a time.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(numbers, n, target)
    for i = 1, n do
        if numbers[i] == target then
            return true
        end
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Search a Score Sheet for a Perfect Score

description.background: After an exam, the teacher wants to know whether anyone in the score sheet scores has received 100 points.
description.task: Search the array for a score of 100.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return true if someone has a score of 100; otherwise return false.
description.hint: The search target is fixed as the perfect score.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] == 100 then
            return true
        end
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Find the Position of a Target Item Count in Backpack Slots

description.background: Each slot in a game backpack stores the count of one item type, and all counts are stored in the array items. The player wants to find the first slot whose count equals target.
description.task: Return the first position whose count equals target.
description.input: One array items, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: If found, return the position; if not found, return 0.
description.hint: After finding the first target, you can return the position directly.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(items, n, target)
    for i = 1, n do
        if items[i] == target then
            return i
        end
    end
    return 0
end

questions.title: Find the First Failing Student in a Retake List

description.background: The teacher stores student scores in the array scores. Students with scores below passLine need to retake the exam, and the teacher wants to find the first student who needs a retake.
description.task: Return the first position whose score is below passLine.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number passLine.
description.output: If a failing student is found, return the position; otherwise return 0.
description.hint: The search condition is whether the score is below the score line.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, passLine)
    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] < passLine then
            return i
        end
    end
    return 0
end

questions.title: Find the Last Perfect-Score Position in Competition Results

description.background: Competition scores are stored in the array scores. Multiple players may have scored 100, and the judge wants to know the position of the last perfect score.
description.task: Return the position of the last 100 score.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: If a score of 100 exists, return the last perfect-score position; otherwise return 0.
description.hint: You can continue traversing the entire array and keep updating the found position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local position = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] == 100 then
            position = i
        end
    end

    return position
end

questions.title: Search for a Target Score and Count How Many Checks Were Made

description.background: The teacher searches a score sheet for target and also wants to know how many scores were checked before finding it.
description.task: Find the first position of target and count the number of checks.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the target position and check count in order. If not found, return 0 for the position and n for the check count.
description.hint: Update the check count every time a position is checked.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, target)
    local position = 0
    local checks = 0
    local i = 1

    while i <= n and position == 0 do
        checks = checks + 1
        if scores[i] == target then
            position = i
        end
        i = i + 1
    end

    return position, checks
end

questions.title: Use Binary Search to Quickly Find a Target Book in Sorted Book Numbers

description.background: Library book numbers are already stored from small to large in the array books. The administrator wants to use a faster method to find target.
description.task: Use binary search to return the target book position and count the number of checks.
description.input: One sorted array books, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the target position and check count in order. If not found, return 0 for the position.
description.hint: Check the middle position each time, then shrink the search range for the next check.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(books, n, target)
    local left = 1
    local right = n
    local position = 0
    local checks = 0

    while left <= right and position == 0 do
        local mid = (left + right) / 2
        if mid % 1 ~= 0 then
            mid = mid - 0.5
        end
        checks = checks + 1

        if books[mid] == target then
            position = mid
        elseif target < books[mid] then
            right = mid - 1
        else
            left = mid + 1
        end
    end

    return position, checks
end

questions.title: Compare How Many Checks Linear Search and Binary Search Need

description.background: The same sorted number list can be searched with linear search or binary search. The teacher wants students to compare how many checks each method uses when searching for target.
description.task: Use linear search and binary search separately to search for target, then return the check counts for both methods.
description.input: One sorted array numbers, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the linear-search check count, binary-search check count, linear-search position, and binary-search position in order. Positions are 0 when not found.
description.hint: Simulate the two methods separately and record each method's check count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(numbers, n, target)
    local linearChecks = 0
    local linearPosition = 0
    local i = 1

    while i <= n and linearPosition == 0 do
        linearChecks = linearChecks + 1
        if numbers[i] == target then
            linearPosition = i
        end
        i = i + 1
    end

    local left = 1
    local right = n
    local binaryChecks = 0
    local binaryPosition = 0

    while left <= right and binaryPosition == 0 do
        local mid = (left + right) / 2
        if mid % 1 ~= 0 then
            mid = mid - 0.5
        end
        binaryChecks = binaryChecks + 1

        if numbers[mid] == target then
            binaryPosition = mid
        elseif target < numbers[mid] then
            right = mid - 1
        else
            left = mid + 1
        end
    end

    return linearChecks, binaryChecks, linearPosition, binaryPosition
end

questions.title: Search a Sorted Score Table for a Target Score or Insertion Position

description.background: The score table scores is already sorted from small to large. The teacher wants to find whether target exists; if it does not exist, the teacher also wants to know where target should be inserted to keep the score table sorted.
description.task: Use binary search to complete the search and return the target state and position.
description.input: One sorted array scores, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: If target is found, return true and the target position; if not found, return false and the insertion position.
description.hint: After the search ends, the remaining boundary position can help determine the insertion position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, target)
    local left = 1
    local right = n
    local position = 0

    while left <= right and position == 0 do
        local mid = (left + right) / 2
        if mid % 1 ~= 0 then
            mid = mid - 0.5
        end

        if scores[mid] == target then
            position = mid
        elseif target < scores[mid] then
            right = mid - 1
        else
            left = mid + 1
        end
    end

    if position > 0 then
        return true, position
    end

    return false, left
end
